context: Emart has 15 ingredient education pages at /ingredients/[slug]. The content JSON at apps/web/src/data/ingredient-content.json has all 15 slugs but each entry averages only ~177 words — far too thin for SEO. Target: 1,500–2,000 words per ingredient. Content renders on a Next.js page above the product grid.

steps.action: For the first 2 slugs (niacinamide + hyaluronic-acid), expand content to 1,500–2,000 words
rules: Write intro: 150–200 words covering what the ingredient is, why it matters for Bangladesh skin
Write 4–5 sections with H2 headings: Benefits, How it works, Who should use it, How to use it in a routine, Products at Emart
Write 5 FAQ entries relevant to Bangladesh shoppers (skin type, climate, layering, safety)
Bangladesh-specific context: humidity, UV exposure, brown/wheatish skin tones, common concerns (acne, dark spots, oiliness)
Mention authentic import and Emart naturally — no spam
NO markdown in body text — plain text only, no ** or ## symbols

steps.action: Report word counts for the 2 expanded entries. Wait for approval before doing remaining 13.
After approval — expand all 15 entries using same template
Write updated JSON to the file (additive — preserve all existing fields, only expand intro/sections/faq values)
steps.safety: Read the file first, update entries in memory, write the full array back. Never lose existing slugs.

steps.action: Deploy: rsync the file to VPS and rebuild

output: Word count per ingredient before/after. Smoke test HTTP status. Flag any content that feels thin or generic.
constraints: SEO freeze: content-only change, no URL/slug changes — safe
Do not change the JSON array structure or field names
Do not touch any Next.js component files
Do not add new slugs — only expand the 15 existing ones
OpenRouter OR credits may be low — use free-tier models: deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash:free, google/gemma-4-31b-it:free
